acceptance_conditions.condition: protocol and source bindings remain exact
full and halving execution counts equal 1,728 and 816
full strategy reproduces all 12 R142 selections
halving strategy reproduces all 12 R143 selections
measured execution-loop runtime reduction is at least 30 percent
halving/full per-execution runtime ratio lies between 0.5 and 2.0
strategy order follows the post-preregistration secret
both strategies use identical circuits, seeds, shots, and backend snapshots
measurement transcript hashes and deterministic outputs verify
cross-calibration, hardware, advantage, BQP, solved-frontier, and credit claims remain false

claim_boundary.what_is_not_supported_even_if_accepted: cross-calibration transfer, real hardware savings, cloud billing savings, protocol soundness, quantum advantage, BQP separation, or solved B4/B8/B10

phase_protocol: commit a fresh secret after public preregistration
derive full-first or halving-first strategy order
perform shared untimed setup and warmup
time each strategy once with perf_counter_ns
write measurement rows before secret reveal
reveal secret and verify transcript hashes and deterministic selections
research_question: Does the accepted R143 charged-execution reduction produce at least thirty percent measured execution-loop wall-clock savings?

shared_untimed_setup: load or compile the same 96 unique-QASM shortlist circuits
one simulator warmup circuit per backend snapshot
timed_scope: update online LCB statistics and eliminate candidates
